desc:
This hidden vertical tunnel rises almost straight up from the area below.
Natural handholds enable you to climb, though slowly.
name: A Natural Stairway

desc:
At this point, the river does end, emptying into a large, underground lake. The
water looks somehow unhealthy, with not a ripple to signify any life.
name: A Dark Lake

desc:
The stream widens considerably here, as well as picking up speed and volume. To
avoid being swept away, you are forced to stay towards the sides of the tunnel,
which continues onward towards the south.
name: An Underground River

desc:
Widening slightly from the east, the stream makes a sharp bend to the east,
sloping downward as well. The water is faster here, due to the increased slope
of the floor. Moss and lichen grow in abundance along glistening rocks.
name: An Underground Stream

desc:
Rising from some incredibly deep spring, a stream of clear water bubbles out
from a hole in the rock floor, trickling its way downwards to the west. Peering
above, this natural cavern's height is immense, reaching as high as the eye can
see.
name: An Underground Stream

desc:
The rock and stone twist and turn into strange patterns and formations, defying
the normal rock patterns seen above.
name: Darkness

desc:
Twisting its way miles below the ground, the passage turns from the east into
another plunge into the inky blackness. To continue now would mean exploring
beneath the populated caves, to whatever lies hidden and undisturbed.
name: Limestone Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel bends from upwards towards the east. The rock here is basalt,
granite, and veins of limestone. The weight of the caves themselves seems to
rest on this rock.
name: Limestone Tunnel

desc:
Water drip-drops intermittently from the ceiling, adding to small, dark pools,
cradled within the jagged stone. Corpses of all sizes are forced up against the
western side of the cave, leaving a trail of blood and gore behind.
name: Rocky Cave

desc:
The natural stone twists and turns its way across the chasm. Oddly enough,
boulders and rocks seem to be scattered across the floor, chiselled and dented.
Blood stains mark the floor and parts of the stone wall, and the echo of the
rushing water is no more.
name: Rocky Passage

desc:
The rough stone arcs its way north and east, caring not for the weary traveller.
The stale air is breathable, if somewhat distasteful. The distant rumbling of
water is all that can be heard.
name: Rocky Bend

desc:
The footing is hard to maintain, and small rocks and debris occasionally fall
harmlessly to the ground. The floor and ceiling of this passage are so rugged
and loose, that a clear path to travel is impossible. Cold air consistently
blasts from below the precipice, eventually brushing off the southern rock face.
name: Rocky Passage

desc:
The ground is tattered and rough, with cracks and crevices. The air is cold and
musty, hinting that this passageway has not been frequently walked upon. As the
tunnel twists to the west, the northern wall falls away, leaving a gaping
precipice, and from far below, a faint sound of passing water is heard.
name: Deep Passage

desc:
The passage levels out more or less here, sloping downwards gently to the south.
The rock here is rough, with stalactites and stalagmites jutting from the stone.
name: Passage into the Depths

desc:
Entering this tunnel, you find it difficult to stop from sliding downwards. With
a little effort, you believe it may be possible to climb up. The angle and
length of this area makes you wonder just how far down it goes.
name: A Deeply Plunging Tunnel

desc:
Entering this room, you notice that the sounds of your passage have faded to
just whispers of what they were. The walls here are carved in a strange way,
where the sounds just disappears into the odd contours. From the south, the
tunnel continues straight and true. The scent of orc is clearly in the air.
name: Silent Tunnel

desc:
This odd tunnel bends at this point from the north to the west. Obscured alcoves
strangely line the walls. The air here is stale, but the faint smell of orc can
be detected. The inhabitants are no longer a mystery, but the purpose of the
destination still is.
name: Bend in the Tunnel

desc:
Here the passageway abruptly ends. The floor is covered with dust and, every so
often, dirt and rock debris fall through cracks on the ceiling. Not a sound can
be heard but the dust falling to the floor.
name: Silent Tunnel

desc:
Morruhk enter Middle-earth at this point. Here, either new Morruhk or dead
Morruhks are called. Only a few distant lamps provide light for the dimly lit
space. This room appears to be deep underground, beyond the reach of all but the
most powerful magic. The massive cavern is reached through a winding path.
name: Halls of the Morruhk

desc:
This enormous cavern stretches out to the west. The ceiling of the entire cavern
vaults upward to a cleverly designed dome that appears to also pull smoke away
from any fires that may be lit here. Strewn bones and mould remnants of food
that litter the hall, which is clearly inhabited.
name: Eastern Edge of the Large Cavern

desc:
Large barrels marked with peculiar markings and stacks of flour are piled up
against the cavern's natural wall. A large cauldron in the corner of the cave
has a shaft right above it that draws the smoke away. The senses are assaulted
by the aroma of some type of meat boiling in the cauldron. Near the cauldron, a
sizable makeshift table is placed.
name: Cauldron in the Cavern

desc:
The southeast portion of the cavern contains a sizable, deep hole that acts as
both a testing ground for prospective warriors and a death pit for slaves and
prisoners who have nothing more to give. It is more difficult to escape this pit
because its edges slope up to the north and west and are surrounded by wooden
barriers.
name: Death Pit in the Cavern

desc:
On the far southern side of this cavern, wooden targets in the shape of elves
and men have been arranged in a line. Weapons missing their targets or glancing
off shields are most likely to blame for the cuts and scratches on the granite
walls.
name: Training Grounds in the Cavern

desc:
Several rusting cages with strong lock bars are fixed on the floor at the
cavern's centre. Some of the cages' walls are welded with chains and shackles
that contain nothing but bones. These are the slaves' living quarters, including
both slaves and captured prisoners. Their labour is employed to mine the
low-grade iron lodes and to excavate new caverns. Here, where they must watch
the rats eat the corpses of their deceased comrades, not a single item of
furniture makes their lives any easier. The majority of the small mounds of
bones are either completely destroyed or broken into tiny fragments.
name: Slave Cages in the Cavern

desc:
In the midst of this cavern's northern wall, a well has been constructed on a
stone dais to provide access to water. A rough opening extending farther into
the mountain has been blackened on the wall to the left of the well. This hole
allows a steady stream of hot air to enter the chamber, warming the area. The
cave gets bigger to the south.
name: Well in the Cavern

desc:
There is a powerful odour in this section of the vast cavern. Some corpses are
stored in large boxes, while others are partially butchered and hung from a row
of pointed meat hooks. Each box's bottom features several holes that allow the
blood to seep out onto the floor. The granite of the northern wall has an uneven
dark brown colour, which seems to be the darkest where a table has been
chiselled out of it.
name: Butchery in the Large Cavern

desc:
The cavern's walls are lined with sturdy beds, and the enormous stone rock in
the middle serves as a table. Furs from various animals are scattered all over
the floor, serving as basic bedding for the cavern's warriors. The quarters have
never been cleaned, and the stench of squalor hangs heavily in the air.
name: Sleeping Area in the Cavern

desc:
The floor is coated in a thick layer of muck, and the damp air is dense with the
scent of decay and mould. The entire cavern has a vaulted ceiling that rises to
a cleverly crafted dome that looks to also draw smoke away from any fires that
might be lit here.
name: Dome in the Large Cavern

desc:
Simple tools and firewood are heaped next to the rough stone forge, which is
located in a depression in the cavern's uneven floor. The forge's fire blazes,
occasionally shooting flares that tint the walls blood-red before fading them
back to black.
name: Stone Forge in the Cavern

desc:
This dark cavern's ceiling is sporadically seared by roaring flames that
occasionally erupt from the enormous fireplace placed against the southern wall.
Anyone who enters the vicinity of the fireplace will feel a bodily blow from the
sweltering heat. Heat and the stench of burning flesh combine to create a foul
odour that hangs heavily in the air. A number of dead, their bodies shattered in
combat, are stacked carelessly next to the fireplace and are ready to be
incinerated by fire.
name: Fireplace in the Corner of the Cavern

desc:
This area of a large cavern is its westernmost portion. A dim light, and
occasionally more smoke than light, is provided by iron brackets that are
fastened to the walls. The smoke may be observed sluggishly ascending to an
oculus positioned in the dome's centre to the east. The cavern's only visible
exit is to the west.
name: Entrance of a Large Cavern

desc:
Every kind of abandoned garbage lines the walls of this caverns intersection. To
create a reasonably clear passage, pieces of broken furniture, spoiled food, and
dirty, muddy brown clothing have all been pushed away. The greatest pieces of
debris have been removed, but the centre of the cavern is still full of thick
mud and worse.
name: Dirty Intersection in a Cavern

desc:
The walls of this cavern are solid, unyielding earth. To the north, where a
grate serves as a barrier, the corridor becomes more stony, while to the south,
it becomes filthy. To the west, the air is thick with the rich smell of fresh
loam and the foul stench of feces.
name: Corridor in a Cavern

desc:
Most of the floor space of this cavern is taken up by a deep pit with a
bowl-like shape. The pit, stained and slippery with ominous substances, smells
of rot and death. The centre of the pit is lined with broken bones, tattered
clothing, and shattering armour. A tunnel of inky blackness leads northward to a
junction.
name: Bowl-like Pit

desc:
This is a significant cavern's westernmost segment. The musty animal smell seems
to be originating from this place. It is noticeably heavier than in the east and
almost tangible. Possibly the ventilation is insufficient, or perhaps the smell
is coming from a nearby source. In front of some cages positioned against the
cavern walls, a dark red liquid that is still moist is visible on the stone
floor.
name: Foul-smelling Cavern

desc:
This small, cramped space has been hewn into the wall of a much larger cavern
outside. The floor of the cavern is damp and grimy, making any sort of firm
footing difficult. The walls and ceiling are bare cavern rock, with scratch
marks in the walls discernible -- both deep and superficial.
name: Snaga Pit
